div.head: 251. Editorial Note
div.p: Harold Macmillan became Prime Minister on January 10, 1957. During the course of 1957, he sent President Eisenhower 54 letters and received 46 from the President. This correspondence covers every major international problem. Many letters dealing with a single subject are printed in the Foreign Relations series in the compilations to which they are relevant; many of the more important which deal with several topics are printed in this compilation. The originals of Macmillan’s letters and copies of the President’s, as well as an index of the whole correspondence, are in the Eisenhower Library, Whitman File, International File. Copies of many of the letters are in Department of State, Presidential Correspondence: Lot 66 D 204.
